verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of mitochondrial F1Fo ATP synthase, canonically resident on the inner mitochondrial membrane. However, multiple independent lines of evidence document an ectopic plasma-membrane pool of ATP5B on the outer leaflet of hepatocytes (as an apoA-I/HDL receptor), endothelial cells, adipocytes, keratinocytes, and on cancer cells (breast, prostate, lung, leukemia), detected by surface biotinylation, flow cytometry, immunogold-EM, and live-cell antibody/aptamer binding. Mechanistically, the assembled complex is trafficked via DRP1/KIF5B and mitochondrial-PM fusion, anchoring eATP synthase on the surface with catalytic activity. This represents cell-state-induced (oncogenic/tissue-specific) outer-leaflet display rather than baseline surface residency.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Surface pool is a minor ectopic fraction; could also be classified as dual_localization given documented PM pool in normal hepatocytes/endothelium.
